```{=html}
<aside class="side-panel inputs-sidebar">
<details>
<summary>About the inputs</summary>

<p><strong>Price process.</strong> π₀ is the carbon price in USD/tonne; P is
the same price in kVCM/tonne (protocol constant). Together they imply
today's spot S<sub>0</sub> = π₀ / P. μ and σ² are the annualised
log-price drift and variance.</p>

<p><strong>Jumps (Merton).</strong> Optional discrete shocks on top of the
GBM. λ<sub>J</sub> is the expected jumps per year; each arrival multiplies
the log-price by N(μ<sub>J</sub>, σ<sub>J</sub>²). The drift is
<em>compensated</em> (μ absorbs −λ<sub>J</sub>·κ with κ = e<sup>μ<sub>J</sub>+σ<sub>J</sub>²/2</sup> − 1)
so E[S<sub>t</sub>] = S<sub>0</sub>·e<sup>μt</sup> stays put — jumps widen
the distribution without relocating it. λ<sub>J</sub> = 0 recovers pure GBM.</p>

<p><strong>Demand &amp; inventory.</strong> Retirement flow λ (tonnes per
calendar day) plus the raw inventory state (k, C<sub>basis</sub>). Coverage
τ = min(1, k / (N·P)) controls the custom book. Three regimes, each with
a different <em>kind</em> of tail:</p>
<ul>
  <li><strong>Under-hedged (τ &lt; 1).</strong> Inventory runs out at τT;
  randomness lives in J<sub>τ</sub>. Loss tail unbounded but softened by
  log-normal averaging.</li>
  <li><strong>Matched (τ = 1, k = N·P).</strong> Custom P&amp;L is
  Q·N − C<sub>basis</sub> — deterministic (§3a).</li>
  <li><strong>Over-hedged (τ = 1, k &gt; N·P).</strong> Leftover
  k<sub>left</sub> tokens mark to spot at S<sub>T</sub>. Loss tail bounded;
  the heavy mass sits on the gain side.</li>
</ul>
<p>Raising C<sub>basis</sub> translates the distribution leftward by the
same amount without touching SD, VaR, or CVaR — basis is a sunk cost.</p>

<p><strong>Custom inventory vs. α.</strong> The Validation page's §3c uses a
single hedge ratio α and assumes inventory was bought today at S<sub>0</sub>.
This page replaces that with (k, C<sub>basis</sub>), recovering the α-case
at k = α·N·P, C<sub>basis</sub> = α·N·P·S<sub>0</sub>. Syndication (β)
likewise applies to the residual stochastic part — the uncovered J<sub>τ</sub>
and, when over-hedged, k<sub>left</sub>·S<sub>T</sub> — and is priced from
this run's MC moments rather than a closed-form shortcut (no clean closed
form exists in the over-hedged regime).</p>

<p><strong>Contract.</strong> Horizon (calendar days in the UI, years
internally), fixed USD quote Q of the principal book, fee rate f of the fee
book. Doubling f doubles both the fee book's mean and its SD, leaving
Sharpe invariant; f does not touch the principal books.</p>

<p><strong>Syndication (§3d).</strong> Quota-share cession of the custom
book's stochastic residual. β is the fraction ceded; θ the counterparty's
risk-load (0 = actuarially fair). Premium mode selects the risk measure θ
multiplies — <code>sharpe</code>: θ·SD; <code>cvar</code>:
θ·SD·φ(Φ<sup>−1</sup>(0.95))/0.05 ≈ 2.063·θ·SD. Retained variance
collapses as (1−β)² on top of the (1−τ)² collapse from coverage.</p>

<p><strong>Switching (§3e).</strong> Flip from the fixed-quote principal
book to the fee book the first time S<sub>t</sub> crosses H = h·S<sub>0</sub>.
h = ∞ disables the switch (§3d baseline); h = 1 starts in fee mode.
f<sub>post</sub> defaults to f but can be raised independently to compensate
for lost upside once the barrier breaches.</p>

</details>
</aside>
```
